transcript.clean_text: the Tour de France steamed on towards the rest day with one final stage before the end of the first block of racing each and every day the race live and On Demand on gcn plus Thunderball and the rest have been protagonists and it was an emotional start for the latter Matilda Paul's grandfather Raymond having been resident in the town where the stage started before his death would be his final destination alongside the other 168 riders in the Peloton one on starter with Quinn Simmons not able to take the start Steph crass and Mark Cavendish had crashed out the race the day before Stage Nine was going to be a huge test a first visit to this Mighty Mountain for the first time in 35 years we were expecting a big fight for the Breakaway Victor campenards pulled out a big group of 14 Riders early on many potential stage winners among them and there was a little bit of a reaction behind Julian ala Philippe launching one of many attacks but here scale mosa will be wondering why Alberto betio wasn't helping him out but in the end the Gap was established 14 up the road and the intermediate Sprint was taken by Uno x one single point remained when Jasper Phillips made sure of it and then the race settled down until 50 kilometers to go just under in fact when Matteo Jorgensen took off clear by this point that the Breakaway was going to take the stage the Gap grew to over 14 minutes Jorgensen having 25 to half a minute and those chasing him having problems including David de la Cruz Clemente had been impressive Michael Woods was in there as was Nielsen Palace and mate mohoric but it was under the start of the climb that Jorgensen the Americans Advantage looked good the worst of the climb the last five kilometers behind it was all about the GC group coming to the bottom of a climb just under a quarter of an hour down where we're gonna have two races at the front mohrich was leaving his companions behind then passed by Woods and woods was closing in amazingly on the front of the race jorgensen's fantastic Advantage disappearing and woods putting on the pressure only taking the lead in the final 400 meters but by this point it was clear that the Canadian was going to pick up a first ever Tour de France stage win celebrating over the top of the mighty mountain and puedom with all the legendary names the likes of caught pijimondi the fight between now had a new winner second place scoring to that tour third as Jorgensen was picked on the line to mate a fantastic fight from the Breakaway and we were gonna have another two from the GC group hindley already distanced by this point pogachar attacking Simon Yates Carlos Rodriguez Pitcock looking good and Jonas vinegar under pressure seconds were starting to eke out and pogacha continuing to claw his way back in the GC 300 meters to go and the Gap was still opening bogacha on the pedals yet again and forcing as much as he could on the 14 gradients he was across the line the clock started that it was a handful of seconds eight seconds in fact as vinegar across the line changing slightly the GC picture going into the rest day peacock was strong Simon Yates too Rodriguez with another solid performance Adam Yates finishing just in front of Jai hindley Henley conserving his third place GC lead to 17. Michael Woods celebrating Israel Premier Tech winning the stage Woods Jorgensen belt in the top five with Paolo and De La Cruz with a GC story also changing slightly we'll get you across the line out of the big favorites and cutting the deficit to 17 seconds after nine stages of the Tour de France the Ingot leads going into the rest tape and there is the updated GC a few changes as well Rodriguez in fourth hindley losing some time Adam Yates fifth his brother Simon in sixth pitcock moving up to seventh go do eighth cuss now in ninth and Homan Baldi dropping two places to tenth it's embalde's home region where the race continues on Tuesday stage 10 a real tough one with an uphill start 167.2 kilometers finishing in Israel and loads of climbing to be done it has Breakaway written all over it but the GC Riders will have to be attentive to make sure nobody dangerous slips away as always you can follow the Tour de France the biggest race in the world live and On Demand on the home of cycling on GCM Plus enjoy your rest day nothing
